summary:
The provided evidence does not directly address behavioral risk factors leading to hypertension transition in Black South Africans, as most studies focus on different ethnic groups such as American Indians. Therefore, without specific data linking behavior to BP changes in this group, any assignment of blame remains speculative—supporting a neutral position that behavioral risks may play a role but lack definitive attribution within current research.\boxed{C}
